title: Palacinky S Plnenom Cesnakom
description: These crumbly Slovakian onion pancakes are the perfect comfort food for a sick day. Serve warm with a dollop of sour cream and some pickled mustard seeds.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, eggs, sour cream, bhuna spice mix, salt, and black pepper.
Grate the yellow onion and add it to the bowl. Mix well to combine.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Brush with melted butter.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the skillet or griddle.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the edges start to curl and the surface is dry.
Loosen the pancake with a spatula and flip it over. Cook for another 1-2 minutes or until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining batter. Serve warm with a dollop of sour cream and some pickled mustard seeds.
Serve immediately, or store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
To reheat, cook in a toaster oven at 375°F (190°C) for 5-7 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
